differences: Before the pass, mobile cards used dense horizontal rows that made content feel clipped at narrow widths.
The top mobile nav intentionally remains horizontally scrollable because it contains many primary destinations.
suggestions: Keep future card headers stacked on mobile before switching to side-by-side layouts at sm or md breakpoints.
Prefer two-column mobile stat grids for numeric summaries, then expand to three or more columns on larger screens.
Use inner overflow-x containers only for genuinely tabular content such as the 18 x 18 type chart.
reasoning: The main page-level overflow risk has been removed, Korean text wrapping is safer, and the densest cards now use mobile-first stacking. Remaining scroll behavior is intentional for navigation and the large type chart.
